UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF GSQUARED RESTAURANT VENTURES, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES. NO SANCTIONS. RESPONDING PARTY WAS JUSTIFIED IN OBJECTING TO THE TERM "INCIDENT" AS DEFINED IN THE FORM INTERROGATORIES; THE TERM WAS SUFFICIENTLY DEFINED IN "MEET AND CONFER" LETTERS AND THIS MOTION. 12.4 AND 12.5: DEFENDANT SHALL IDENTIFY ALL PHOTOGRAPHS, FILMS, VIDEOTAPES, DIAGRAMS, REPRODUCTIONS AND MODELS MADE FROM NOVEMBER 2007 TO FEBRUARY 2011 DEPICTING AREAS TENANT WAS SUPPOSED TO BE ENTITLED TO USE UNDER THE LEASE. IF SUCH DOCUMENTS WERE PRODUCED IN REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION THEY MUST BE IDENTIFIED BY BATES NUMBER OR BY NAME; 12.6: DEFENDANT SHALL IDENTIFY ALL REPORTS INCLUDING INFORMAL AND PRIVILEGED REPORTS. IF SUCH DOCUMENTS WERE PRODUCED IN REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION THEY MUST BE IDENTIFIED BY BATES NUMBER OR BY NAME; 14.1: DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES BASED ON DEFINITION OF "INCIDENT" AS PROVIDED IN PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT 3:14-19. ALSO, PRIVILEGE IS NOT APPLICABLE IF ACTUAL CONTENTION IS BEING MADE. 50.6: DEFENDANT SHALL RESPOND TO THIS INTERROGATORY. READING AND ANALYZING A DOCUMENT THAT FORMS THE BASIS OF THIS LITIGATION IS NOT UNREASONABLE OR OVERLY BURDENSOME. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: HARRY DORFMAN, CLERK: MARS GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Against Pltf Gsquared Restaurant Ventures, Llc
Off Calendar May-29-2014 Continued To Jun-30-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jason M. Skaggs, Esq. (650-617-3226), attorney for plaintiff/opposing party
K. Nicole Buck, Esq. of Kaye Scholer LLP (650-319-4507), attorney for defendant/moving party Ghirardelli Acquisition Company

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court adopts tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT GHIRARDELLI ACQUISITION COMPANY's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Gsquared Restaurant Ventures, LLC is Continued to June 30, 2014. Gary Danko shall appear for additional deposition so that Defendant may explore his assertions regarding facts forming the basis of responses to Form Interrogatories. Rifkind v. Sup. Ct (1994) 22 al.App.4th 1255. Deposition shall take place at a mutually convenient time no later than June 18, 2014. Plaintiff shall pay costs and reasonable attorney's fees incurred for the deposition. Defendant shall file and serve a declaration no later than June 24, 2014 regarding costs and fees and supplemental brief (not to exceed 5 pages), if any, regarding the outcome of the deposition. Plaintiff may file a response (not to exceed 5 pages), if any, no later than June 26, 2014. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT GHIRARDELLI ACQUISITION COMPANY, LLC's Motion For Protective Order is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden and show that disclosure of information regarding past leases of the property, which GAC does not own anymore, constitutes unwarranted annoyance to GAC or to the new owner of the property. CCP 2031.060(b). Prevailing party to submit order approved as to form. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories
Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott J. McKay of McKay & Leong (925-932-6095) appearing through Court Call for GSquared Restaurant Ventures , LLC a California Limited Liability Company, Plaintiff/Cross Defendant and moving party.
Lauren S. Kowal., Esq of Coblentz Patch Duffy & Bass LLP (415-772-5711) for Defendant, Cross Complainant and Opposing Party Cocoa Development REtail, LLC A Delaware Limited Liability Company.

ARGUED. Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:PLAINTIFF GSQUARED RESTAURANT VENTURES, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories is GRANTED. Defendant shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to provide further responses. No sanctions. Responding party was justified in objecting to the term "INCIDENT" as defined in the Form Interrogatories; the term was sufficiently defined in "meet and confer" letters and this motion.
12.4 and 12.5: Defendant shall identify all photographs, films, videotapes, diagrams, reproductions and models made from November 2007 to February 2011 depicting areas tenant was supposed to be entitled to use under the lease. If such documents were produced in Request for Production they must be identified by bates number or by name;
12.6: Defendant shall identify all reports including informal and privileged reports. If such documents were produced in Request for Production they must be identified by bates number or by name;
14.1: Defendant shall provide further responses based on definition of "INCIDENT" as provided in Plaintiff's Separate Statement 3:14-19. Also, Privilege is not applicable if actual contention is being made.
50.6: Defendant shall respond to this interrogatory. Reading and analyzing a document that forms the basis of this litigation is not unreasonable or overly burdensome.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order approved as to form. Judge: Harry Dorfman, Clerk: Mars Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
